title: Lamb Ouzi Whole Slow Roasted

seo.title: Authentic Lamb Ouzi Recipe | Whole Slow Roasted
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of authentic Middle Eastern Lamb Ouzi. Our whole slow roasted lamb recipe is perfect for a special dinner. Get step-by-step instructions!
longTailKeywords: whole slow roasted lamb ouzi recipe
seo.h1: Authentic Lamb Ouzi: Whole Slow Roasted Recipe
h2Headings: Ingredients for Whole Slow Roasted Lamb Ouzi
Tips for Perfect Slow Roasted Lamb Ouzi
seo.imageAlt: A beautifully presented whole slow roasted Lamb Ouzi dish with rice and nuts, ready to serve.
seo.imageCaption: Indulge in the rich flavors of Middle Eastern cuisine with our exquisite whole slow roasted Lamb Ouzi.

description: Experience the rich tapestry of Middle Eastern flavors with our exquisite Lamb Ouzi. This dish combines tender, spiced lamb pieces with aromatic rice and toasted nuts for a truly unforgettable meal. Perfect for a festive dinner, it brings the warmth and hospitality of the region to your table with a quicker preparation.

ingredients.notes: or mix of cinnamon, cardamom, allspice

instructions.title: Prepare the Lamb and Aromatics
instructions.text: Season the lamb cubes generously with half of the ouzi spice blend, salt, and black pepper. In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat the ghee or olive oil over medium-high heat. Add the chopped onion and saute until softened, about 3-4 minutes. Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant.

instructions.title: Brown the Lamb
instructions.text: Add the seasoned lamb cubes to the pot. Increase heat if necessary and brown the lamb on all sides, stirring occasionally. This step helps to seal in the juices and develop a rich flavor. Cook for about 5-7 minutes until the lamb is nicely seared.

instructions.title: Add Rice and Spices
instructions.text: Drain the soaked basmati rice and add it to the pot with the lamb and aromatics. Stir well to combine, ensuring the rice is coated with the flavors. Add the remaining ouzi spice blend and stir for about 1 minute until the spices are fragrant.

instructions.title: Introduce Broth and Vegetables
instructions.text: Pour in the hot chicken or lamb broth. Stir gently to mix everything. Add the frozen peas and diced carrots (if using). Bring the mixture to a boil over high heat.

instructions.title: Simmer to Perfection
instructions.text: Once boiling, reduce the heat to low, cover the pot tightly with a lid, and let it simmer for 15-20 minutes, or until all the liquid has been absorbed and the rice is tender. Do not lift the lid during this time.

instructions.title: Rest, Fluff, and Serve
instructions.text: Once cooked, remove the pot from the heat and let it rest, covered, for another 5 minutes. This allows the steam to redistribute and the rice to become perfectly fluffy. Uncover, fluff the rice gently with a fork, then transfer to a large serving platter. Garnish generously with toasted slivered almonds, pine nuts, and fresh chopped parsley. Serve hot.

tips: To achieve a truly authentic Ouzi flavor, use a homemade Ouzi spice blend with freshly ground cardamom, cinnamon, allspice, and a touch of nutmeg.
For extra richness, brown the lamb cubes in a mixture of butter and olive oil. If you don't have lamb broth, chicken broth works well, or even water with a bouillon cube.
Ouzi is best served immediately. Leftovers can be stored in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days. Reheat gently with a splash of broth to maintain moisture.
article:
Mastering the art of Middle Eastern cuisine often involves dishes that are as rich in history and culture as they are in flavor. Among these culinary treasures, one stands out as a true centerpiece, a dish revered for its impressive presentation and melt-in-your-mouth tenderness: Lamb Ouzi Whole Slow Roasted. This celebratory meal, with its aromatic spices and succulent meat, promises an unforgettable dining experience, transforming any gathering into a grand feast.

## What is Lamb Ouzi?

Lamb Ouzi, often spelled "Qoozi" or "Ghozi," is a quintessential Middle Eastern dish, particularly prominent in the Gulf region and Levant. It traditionally features a whole lamb or a significant portion, such as a leg or shoulder, slow-roasted until it reaches an extraordinary level of tenderness, practically falling off the bone. What elevates Lamb Ouzi beyond a simple roast is its aromatic accompaniment: a spiced rice pilaf, often studded with nuts, dried fruits, and sometimes ground meat. While some elaborate versions involve deboning the lamb and stuffing it with this flavorful rice, most home cooks and restaurants serve the magnificent slow-roasted lamb alongside a generous mound of the fragrant Ouzi rice. It's a dish synonymous with hospitality, special occasions, and family celebrations, embodying the warmth and generosity of Middle Eastern culture.

## Ingredients for Whole Slow Roasted Lamb Ouzi

To create an authentic and memorable Whole Slow Roasted Lamb Ouzi, selecting quality ingredients is paramount. This recipe focuses on a large leg or shoulder of lamb, which is more manageable for home kitchens than an entire lamb.

**For the Lamb:**
*   **Lamb:** 1 large leg of lamb (bone-in, approximately 5-7 lbs / 2.3-3.2 kg) or a large lamb shoulder.
*   **Olive Oil:** 1/4 cup, for the marinade.
*   **Lemon Juice:** 2 tablespoons, fresh.
*   **Plain Yogurt (optional):** 1/4 cup, for tenderizing and richness in the marinade.
*   **Garlic:** 6-8 cloves, minced.
*   **Fresh Ginger:** 1-inch piece, grated (optional).
*   **Salt:** 2-3 teaspoons (adjust to taste and lamb size).
*   **Black Pepper:** 1 teaspoon, freshly ground.
*   **Ground Cumin:** 2 teaspoons.
*   **Ground Coriander:** 2 teaspoons.
*   **Paprika:** 1 teaspoon (smoked or sweet).
*   **Ground Turmeric:** 1 teaspoon.
*   **Ground Cardamom:** 1 teaspoon.
*   **Ground Cinnamon:** 1/2 teaspoon.
*   **Allspice:** 1/2 teaspoon.
*   **Water or Lamb/Chicken Broth:** 2-3 cups, for the roasting pan.

**For the Ouzi Rice (Serve alongside):**
*   **Basmati Rice:** 2 cups, rinsed thoroughly.
*   **Olive Oil or Ghee:** 2 tablespoons.
*   **Onion:** 1 medium, finely chopped.
*   **Garlic:** 2 cloves, minced.
*   **Ground Lamb or Beef (optional):** 1/2 lb (225g), if desired for a richer rice.
*   **Spices:** 1 teaspoon ground cumin, 1 teaspoon ground coriander, 1/2 teaspoon ground turmeric, 1/4 teaspoon ground cinnamon, 1/4 teaspoon allspice.
*   **Salt:** 1 teaspoon (or to taste).
*   **Black Pepper:** 1/2 teaspoon.
*   **Chicken or Lamb Broth:** 3 cups.
*   **Pine Nuts:** 1/4 cup, lightly toasted.
*   **Slivered Almonds:** 1/4 cup, lightly toasted.
*   **Raisins or Dried Apricots (chopped):** 1/4 cup (optional).
*   **Fresh Parsley or Mint:** 1/4 cup, chopped, for garnish.

## Step-by-Step Instructions for Lamb Ouzi

Preparing Whole Slow Roasted Lamb Ouzi is a labor of love, but the steps are straightforward and the reward is immense.

1.  **Prepare the Lamb Marinade:** In a small bowl, combine olive oil, lemon juice, optional yogurt, minced garlic, grated ginger, salt, black pepper, cumin, coriander, paprika, turmeric, cardamom, cinnamon, and allspice. Mix well to form a paste.
2.  **Prepare the Lamb:** Pat the leg of lamb dry with paper towels. Using a sharp knife, score the fatty side of the lamb in a diamond pattern, about 1/2 inch deep. Make several deep incisions (about 1-2 inches deep) all over the lamb with the tip of your knife. This allows the marinade to penetrate deeply.
3.  **Marinate the Lamb:** Rub the prepared spice paste generously all over the lamb, ensuring it gets into all the scores and incisions. Place the lamb in a large roasting pan or a large zip-top bag. Cover and refrigerate for at least 8 hours, or preferably overnight (up to 24 hours), to allow the flavors to meld and tenderize the meat.
4.  **Roast the Lamb (Low and Slow):** Remove the lamb from the refrigerator about 1-2 hours before roasting to bring it closer to room temperature. Preheat your oven to 325°F (160°C). Place the marinated lamb on a rack in a large roasting pan. Pour 2-3 cups of water or broth into the bottom of the pan – this creates steam, keeping the lamb moist. Tightly cover the roasting pan with heavy-duty aluminum foil or a lid.
5.  **Initial Roasting:** Roast for 4-5 hours, or until the lamb is incredibly tender. The exact time will depend on the size of your lamb and your oven. A good indicator is when the meat easily pulls away from the bone.
6.  **Crisp the Exterior:** Remove the foil and increase the oven temperature to 375°F (190°C). Roast for another 30-45 minutes, or until the exterior is beautifully browned and slightly crispy. An internal temperature of 145°F (63°C) for medium-rare, or higher for fall-apart tender, is ideal.
7.  **Rest the Lamb:** Once cooked, remove the lamb from the oven, transfer it to a large cutting board, and tent loosely with foil. Let it rest for at least 20-30 minutes. This step is crucial for the juices to redistribute, ensuring a tender and succulent roast.
8.  **Prepare the Ouzi Rice:** While the lamb is resting, prepare the rice. Heat olive oil or ghee in a large pot or Dutch oven over medium heat. Sauté chopped onion until softened, about 5 minutes. Add minced garlic and optional ground meat (if using) and cook until the meat is browned. Stir in the rice spices (cumin, coriander, turmeric, cinnamon, allspice) and cook for 1 minute until fragrant. Add the rinsed Basmati rice and stir to coat. Pour in the broth and salt, bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 15-18 minutes, or until the liquid is absorbed and the rice is fluffy.
9.  **Finish the Rice:** Turn off the heat and let the rice stand, covered, for 10 minutes. Fluff with a fork, then gently stir in the toasted pine nuts, almonds, and optional raisins or apricots.
10. **Serve:** Carve or pull apart the succulent Lamb Ouzi. Serve generous portions alongside the fragrant Ouzi rice, garnished with fresh parsley or mint.

## Tips for Perfect Slow Roasted Lamb Ouzi

Achieving perfection with your Slow Roasted Lamb Ouzi requires a few key considerations:

*   **Quality Lamb:** Start with a good quality, fresh leg or shoulder of lamb. Bone-in cuts generally offer more flavor and moisture.
*   **Marination Time is Key:** Do not rush the marination. The longer the lamb sits in the spice mixture, the more flavor it absorbs, and the more tender it becomes. Overnight is truly best.
*   **Low and Slow:** The secret to fall-off-the-bone tender lamb is a low oven temperature and a long cooking time. This breaks down the tough connective tissues gradually.
*   **Maintain Moisture:** Covering the roasting pan tightly with foil and adding liquid to the bottom prevents the lamb from drying out during its long roast.
*   **Resting Period:** This cannot be stressed enough. Resting allows the muscle fibers to relax and reabsorb the juices, resulting in a more succulent and flavorful roast.
*   **Meat Thermometer:** For precise cooking, use a meat thermometer. While the goal is usually well-done and pull-apart for Ouzi, knowing the internal temperature helps ensure it's cooked through without being overdone and dry.
*   **Toasting Nuts:** Toasting the pine nuts and almonds for the Ouzi rice brings out their full flavor and adds a delightful crunch.

## Serving Suggestions for Lamb Ouzi

Lamb Ouzi is a feast in itself, but it shines even brighter when paired with complementary sides that enhance its rich flavors.

*   **The Ouzi Rice:** This is non-negotiable! The spiced rice pilaf is the traditional and perfect accompaniment, soaking up the lamb's juices.
*   **Yogurt Sauce:** A simple, cooling yogurt sauce (such as labneh or plain Greek yogurt thinned with a little water, seasoned with garlic, mint, and a pinch of salt) provides a refreshing contrast to the rich lamb.
*   **Fresh Salads:** Crisp, tangy Middle Eastern salads like Fattoush (with toasted pita bread, mixed greens, and a sumac vinaigrette) or Tabbouleh (a fresh parsley and bulgur wheat salad) offer a bright, herbaceous counterpoint.
*   **Pickled Vegetables:** A selection of Middle Eastern pickles (like pickled turnips, cucumbers, or peppers) adds a sharp, briny note that cuts through the richness.
*   **Warm Flatbreads:** Freshly baked pita bread or other regional flatbreads are perfect for scooping up the lamb and rice.
*   **Garnish:** A generous sprinkling of fresh parsley, mint, and additional toasted nuts (pine nuts, almonds) adds visual appeal and fresh flavor.

## Variations & Substitutions

While the classic Lamb Ouzi is magnificent, there are several ways to adapt it to your preferences or available ingredients:

*   **Lamb Cut:** If a whole leg or shoulder is too much, smaller cuts like a boneless lamb roast or even lamb shanks can be slow-roasted with similar spices, though cooking times will vary.
*   **Rice Stuffing:** Instead of serving the rice alongside, you can debone a lamb shoulder or leg, partially cook the spiced rice, stuff it into the cavity, and then tie and roast the lamb. This requires more skill but is incredibly impressive.
*   **Spice Blends:** Experiment with regional spice blends like Baharat or Za'atar in your marinade for different flavor profiles. A pinch of dried rose petals or mahlab can add an authentic floral note.
*   **Tenderizing Agents:** If you don't use yogurt, a splash of vinegar or even a grated kiwi (for a very short marinade time) can help tenderize the lamb.
*   **Cooking Method:** While traditional slow roasting is preferred, smaller cuts can be adapted for a slow cooker or even a pressure cooker for tender results in a shorter timeframe, though the crispy exterior will need to be achieved separately.
*   **Vegetarian Ouzi Rice:** For a vegetarian option, omit the ground meat in the rice and focus on intensifying the spices and adding more vegetables like carrots or peas.

Embarking on the journey to create your own Lamb Ouzi Whole Slow Roasted is a culinary adventure that promises rich rewards. The aroma filling your kitchen, the tender lamb melting on your tongue, and the vibrant flavors of the Middle East will transport you and your guests. It’s more than just a meal; it’s an experience, a celebration of flavor, family, and tradition. So, gather your ingredients, take your time, and prepare to impress with this magnificent Middle Eastern masterpiece.
